creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that transforms 10,000+ notes, documents, and repositories from an Obsidian vault into live context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture with raw content, indexed knowledge, and a wiki layer to enable efficient token-based memory without relying on vector or graph databases.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a technical talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture patterns, evaluation methods, monitoring approaches, tool integration, and engineering practices learned from deploying agents across thousands of state and local governments.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents suffer from severe limitations: they can only view small portions of codebases and lose context between interactions, making them ineffective despite individual competence. He frames this as analogous to a master engineer with amnesia and proposes that solutions like Nx and Polygraph can address these constraints.

videos.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
videos.summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent infrastructure should be built around durable, append-only session logs rather than orchestration layers and tools. He contends that as models commoditize, the log becomes the differentiating primitive that persists across models and runtimes, enabling properties like durability, continuity, and portability.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of results while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the other 90%. He frames AI coding as a spectrum from vibe coding to structured agentic engineering and discusses how the role of engineers is shifting from conductor to orchestrator.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a cross-agent memory layer that persists context outside individual AI tools. It stores encrypted memories owned by the user and allows different AI agents from different providers to access shared context without knowledge of each other.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. Cole Medin reports that the model delivers impressive performance relative to its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Advanced engineers use agentic loops to automatically re-prompt AI models iteratively, reducing manual prompting but increasing costs and compounding errors. The pattern involves an orchestrator agent spinning up worker agents in parallel, with observability infrastructure to monitor token usage and hallucinations over long-running executions.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason showcases a new project involving Crabbox and a codebase harness skill infrastructure available on GitHub. The project is part of the AI Builder Club community and includes a Superdesign component for development workflows.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer, a technique for setting up and building AI agents. The video includes free resources, a GitHub repository with templates, and workshop materials for learning the approach in practice.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A guide on constructing proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through AI loops. Covers autonomous optimization use cases in ads and SEO, with tooling via Loopany, memory systems, and agent tools as enabling components.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, positioning them as the next evolution beyond Ralph-loop. The video covers goal-setting frameworks, mission exploration for horizontal task execution, and tools like Crewlet and Superdesign for autonomous code generation and design.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Educational video explaining how generative pretrained transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video walks through the fundamental mechanics of GPT architecture that powers models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: A YouTube video by Caleb Writes Code that explains Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model in 10 minutes. The video walks through tokenization, embeddings, training, and backpropagation to build foundational understanding of how language models process tokens, positioning bigrams as a prerequisite for understanding attention and GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, Latent MoE, and MTP components to differentiate itself from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model implementing MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements to reduce latency in prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a 7-step framework for system design interviews that prioritizes problem understanding before technology selection. The framework covers requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints, demonstrated through a URL shortener example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: AWS Developers explains how AI agents use tools to move beyond static chatbot behavior and access real-time data. The video covers tool architecture, tool calling workflows, system prompts for tool selection, and building custom tools from scratch using Strands Agents.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails to work with AWS services effectively. It replaces reliance on general agent knowledge with up-to-date, tested procedures that improve success rates.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code, an agentic coding tool by Anthropic, on Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure for compliance and billing reasons. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing whether Google engineers use AI code generation tools, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, and examining Google's internal approach to code generation adoption in 2024-2026. The analysis frames code generation adoption in terms of how engineers perceive and embrace these tools.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or product perceived as disruptive to open source software. The video examines how Malus operates and its impact on open source maintainers, framing it as potentially transformative for the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone is insufficient as a software moat; customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale provide competitive advantage. He contends that fears of AI eliminating SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products create highly engaged customers rather than merely satisfied ones.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the robotics industry's focus on humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree is myopic. She contends that future robotics should prioritize human interaction and delight in design rather than utility alone, anticipating a major inflection point for robotics similar to ChatGPT's impact on AI.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how a Paris-based AI performance management platform competes against established enterprise software giants. She covers why legacy tools like spreadsheets fail at scale for large enterprises, the advantages of building a full platform from day one, and strategies for winning trust with marquee customers including Figma, Brex, and Anthropic.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customers are not proof of real product-market fit and shares contrarian advice on identifying genuine market pull over false signals. He advocates embedding in customer workflows and Slack channels instead of formal interviews, and emphasizes making AI bets on future model capabilities rather than current constraints.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof highlights overlooked security threats in agentic AI systems, including prompt injection attacks, unauthorized data deletion, and unintended actions. The video argues that businesses are hesitant to deploy agentic AI due to these security risks despite its potential benefits.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's 'This Is Tech Year' podcast episode discusses Anthropic's latest developments with guest Joao. The episode combines technology news coverage with local culture references to Lisbon and the Santos festival.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams achieve higher productivity and task completion compared to large teams, which become inefficient due to extensive meeting overhead. The takeaway is that smaller organizational units tend toward greater efficiency.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses rising demand for high bandwidth memory driven by AI applications. Micron and SK Hynix are positioned as major players shaping the future of memory technology for AI workloads.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
